title: Memory
content:
One had a lovely face,
And two or three had charm,
But charm and face were in vain
Because the mountain grass
Cannot but keep the form
Where the mountain hare has lain.
meaning:
William Butler Yeats's contribution to classic is further solidified by the brilliance found in "Memory"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
